# Questions in Livestream 150

Live Stream #150: Not So Glorious Glass Fish

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=565" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:25</a> | What was the Egyptian word for "wheel," and what is the oldest text or inscription that bears it? | He gives the Egyptian term and says the oldest text he found is a Dynasty 19 Deir el-Medina ostracon, though chariots themselves are attested earlier. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=662" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:02</a> | How can Galatians 4:25 place Sinai in Arabia and still fit a Sea of Reeds crossing? | He says Roman-era Arabia included Sinai, so Paul's wording still fits a Sinai setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=855" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:15</a> | How did ancient Egyptians first develop their ideas about the afterlife? | He says the exact development is unknown, but it seems to grow from a belief that humans survive death and from early Osiris-related mortuary ideas at Abydos. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=1047" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:27</a> | How does 1 Kings 9:26 using "Sea of Reeds" fit your location for the Exodus crossing? | He says the name was reused for multiple bodies of water, and this verse is referring to the real Red Sea at Aqaba rather than the Exodus crossing site. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=1413" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:33</a> | What advice would you give someone helping a doubting spouse? | He says doubt is not automatically bad; a tested faith can be healthier than an untested one, provided doubt does not harden into excess. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=1899" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:39</a> | What do you think of Scott Stripling's early date for Joshua's altar based on pottery and scarabs? | He says commemorative scarabs were a real phenomenon, so they must be handled carefully, but he still thinks the Mount Ebal scarabs fit the nineteenth dynasty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=2229" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:09</a> | Do you think the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki are historical? | He says they are important historical sources even though, like many ancient chronicles, they contain monarchic bias. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=2441" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:41</a> | Can concepts like kami, enlightenment, Mandate of Heaven, or related Asian religious ideas be recontextualized for Christianity? | He says some terms could be redefined cautiously, but core meanings often conflict with Christian categories and cannot simply be imported unchanged. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=2804" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:44</a> | Could Semitic and Indo-European similarities point to a deeper common ancestor? | He declines to answer confidently, saying historical linguistics of that sort is outside his expertise. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=2931" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:51</a> | Who decides which doctrines are core to Christianity, and is excluding groups like Jehovah's Witnesses a no-true-Scotsman fallacy? | He says the no-true-Scotsman charge does not apply when the disputed traits are actually relevant to the definition of Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=3383" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:23</a> | Does `harpazo` in 1 Thessalonians 4:17-18 imply the rapture? | He says the passage is about believers being caught up to meet the Lord, but must be read in its context rather than turned into a detached rapture scheme. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=3647" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:47</a> | What overlooked fact about Paul's journeys stands out? | He says he has not studied Paul's journeys deeply, but notes an episode where Paul seems to be rejected and beaten without getting to preach much first. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=3786" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:06</a> | What are your general thoughts on fascism and national socialism? | He calls them evil and frames them as dangerous variants of collectivist political ideology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=4100" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:20</a> | Why do many low-content critics mock your laugh or style? | He says these objections usually avoid the argument itself and focus on irrelevant presentation details. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=4987" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:07</a> | What are the main lessons from Obadiah? | He summarizes Obadiah as a prophecy against Edom, warning that pride and hostility toward Judah would be judged. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=5262" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:42</a> | Is Adam living 700,000 years ago compatible with Genesis? | He says that tying Adam specifically to 700,000 years ago does not seem like a strong reading, while older humanity in Genesis 1 is a different question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=5508" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:48</a> | How would the Egyptians have viewed the ten plagues? | He says they would likely see them as intensified Nile-related events and as signs that the people had become weak or decadent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=5645" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:05</a> | Have you looked into Yosef Garfinkel's findings related to the Davidic kingdom, and do his conclusions have merit? | He says Garfinkel's case is interesting and often unfairly attacked, and he thinks it supports a broader Davidic kingdom than minimalists allow. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=5861" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:41</a> | Is biblical maximalism an insult in biblical studies, and how respected is the position? | He says it is often used pejoratively, but it is not inherently an insult or a faith claim; it describes an approach to the biblical texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=5991" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:51</a> | Is Gary Rendsburg religious? | He says yes, while also noting that Rendsburg holds some views he regards as relatively secular in scholarship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=6242" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:02</a> | Why are we here, and what is the point of living? | He answers with Ecclesiastes: remember the Creator, know God, enjoy God, and live in relationship with him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=6443" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:23</a> | In Judges 3:15, what significance did Ehud being left-handed have in the ancient Near East? | He says it was not an omen, but left-handedness could exclude someone from normal military service and made Ehud's concealed weapon unexpected. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=6804" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:24</a> | What is your favorite sword? | He favors Japanese swords, especially wakizashi and some later makers, and recommends looking at high-quality Japanese sword work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=7066" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:46</a> | In Mark 8:23, why did Jesus use those specific actions in healing the blind man? | He says Jesus used different methods, and parabolic actions like spittle or mud communicated something and helped observers recognize the healing as real. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=7420" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:40</a> | Is it significant that Exodus and Wilderness narratives do not depend on one Egyptian loanword carrying most occurrences? | He says the Exodus corpus is broader and uses a wider range of Egyptian vocabulary, which strengthens the case for an Egyptian background. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=7654" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:34</a> | Is the Expanded Bible a good translation? | He has not read enough to judge it fully, but says it seems like a modern Amplified Bible approach and would require extended use before review. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=7830" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:30</a> | In Matthew 24:36, what about the Holy Spirit knowing the day or hour? | He says the Holy Spirit is outside the passage's immediate scope, so the text does not give a clear direct answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=8233" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:13</a> | Are you still teaching outside YouTube, and is there evidence of Egyptian decline after the sea crossing? | He says he now works full-time in content creation and writing, and he points to late-Ramesside military decline as part of the larger picture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=8484" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:24</a> | What do you think of C. S. Lewis's Space Trilogy? | He found it difficult to read because of its pacing and dispassionate point-of-view style, though he understands Lewis's theological aim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=9279" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:39</a> | Where can someone find your Rohl debate, and does Titus Kennedy have good archaeology work? | He says the debate papers are available from WordMp3 but not the full question period, and he is critical of Kennedy's Old Testament archaeology work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=10364" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:44</a> | Is it sinful to play a game where you fight against Yahweh? | He says he would avoid it; even if someone argued it was permissible, he does not think it is beneficial or spiritually wise. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=10656" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:36</a> | Do you have any anime recommendations? | He recommends several classic or notable anime, including Akira, Death Note, Monster, and an Eden-titled series, while noting some are disturbing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=10882" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:22</a> | What was the population of Egypt at the time of the Exodus? | He gives an estimate of about two million people for Egypt as a whole. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=10960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:40</a> | Was incest common in Egypt? | He says no; close-kin marriage was rare outside royal contexts, and many supposed brother-sister references are terms of endearment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=11154" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:54</a> | Why is the blood in Exodus 7:19 associated with blackness like battlefield blood? | He says ancient people associated exposed blood with blackness because blood oxidizes and darkens quickly outside the body. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=11471" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:11</a> | If the plagues are not judgments on Egyptian gods, what do they signify? | He says they are judgments on Pharaoh and Egypt for oppression and refusal, not primarily on deities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=11648" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:08</a> | How should Leviticus 10:1-2 be understood, and why was the fire profane? | He treats Nadab and Abihu's act as a double offense involving improper handling of the ark's poles and fire from a ritually questionable source. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=12202" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:22</a> | What are your thoughts on the Eth Cepher Bible? | He has not read enough to review it, but says some readings and Hebrew-style renderings he has seen look questionable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=12564" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:24</a> | Is your NET Bible first or second edition? | He checks and says it is the second edition, then explains why a physical Bible matters for serious review. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=12781" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:01</a> | Is Mount Sinai named after the peninsula, or the peninsula after the mountain, and how does Horeb fit? | He says Horeb and Sinai are synonyms, and the mountain name is tied to the Sinai peninsula. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=12824" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:44</a> | Did the high priest wear bells or a rope in case he died in the Holy of Holies? | He says no; the garment made sound, but the death-rope story is not how the ritual worked. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=13301" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:41</a> | What are some problems with postmillennialism? | He objects to the idea that human society will become good enough for Christ to return, saying that does not fit his view of human sinfulness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=13488" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:48</a> | Which church fathers should someone read to defend God as transcendent rather than omnipresent? | He says he does not know a church-father source for that specific issue and points instead to a William Lane Craig article as relevant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=13611" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:51</a> | Is there anything you would like prayer for? | He asks for prayer for the channel and for health, saying the work can be discouraging and difficult at times. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=13703" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:23</a> | How is the book proposal coming along, and what is it for? | He says it is an Exodus book proposal looking at the Exodus through Egyptian eyes, with one publisher interested while he waits on sales information. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=13874" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:14</a> | Where can someone get the Samaritan Pentateuch or Qumran works? | He recommends Step Bible for accessible Samaritan Pentateuch material and says the Qumran material is best represented in the large Discoveries in the Judaean Desert series. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=14052" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:12</a> | How was the spam jerky? | He says it was delicious and turned out very well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=14135" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:35</a> | What is your favorite ancient Egyptian holiday or festival? | He chooses the Festival of the Valley because it was a people's festival involving tomb visits, meals with ancestors, and processions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=14354" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:14</a> | Are the New Testament and Jesus funnier in Greek? | He says yes, especially Thomas, whose sarcasm and phrasing come through more clearly in Greek. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=14940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:09:00</a> | How should early Japanese figures like Himiko, Amaterasu, Yamato Takeru, Jingu, and Jimmu be treated historically? | He says some belong in a myth-history category, where an historical core may be mixed with divine or legendary material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=15159" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:12:39</a> | What is your beef with the French? | He frames it as Canadian regional rivalry and jokes about being taught Quebec French, which did not go well in Paris. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=15271" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:14:31</a> | Could the Egyptian throne skip generations if the eldest son died before Pharaoh? | He says generally no; a grandson would not inherit through someone who never held the throne, so succession would usually move to another eligible son. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=15380" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:16:20</a> | What is your opinion of Free Grace theology? | He treats it as a version of once-saved-always-saved that can become problematic if it separates salvation from repentance and discipleship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=15914" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:25:14</a> | How is Genesis 1 analogous to a temple dedication or temple framework? | He says Genesis 1 works as temple theology, with ordered sacred spaces and humanity placed as God's image-bearing presence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=16207" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:30:07</a> | What is the purpose of concubines in the Old Testament, and was David's use of them immoral? | He says concubinage was regulated rather than approved, comparing it to practices the Old Testament manages legally without endorsing as ideal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/I53p5iUeVjY?t=16719" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:38:39</a> | What is the theme of the book of Judges? | He says Judges is arranged didactically from good judges to bad judges, teaching Israel to recognize faithful and unfaithful leadership. |
